subject: Re: problem with subtomogram reconstruction after conversion from Dynamo
body:
HI Anna,
I have followed your thread on the dynamo forum and I must admit i do
not have very much to add to the suggestions that Alister has already
given. I would however try two things -
1. After subtomogram extraction, could you map the coordinates from
the star file (you have already excluded Dynamo at this point) of the
sub tomograms back into the tomogram? Do those coordinates land on the
lattice? The dynamo conversion package is fairly bullet-proof and I am
not suspecting a problem within the package, however, this exercise
might help rule out operator errors and confirm that indeed the
sub-volumes actually enclose the lattice.
2. You mentioned that you performed "the first averaging step in
relion" which led to a worse looking volume than when you did
something similar in Dynamo. While I do not necessarily understand
what you mean by the phrase you used, I would suggest that you perform
a reconstruction following subtomogram reconstruction (see codeblock
here for reference http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=169). This test
will try to get to the bottom of whether the orientation priors
generated in dynamo continue to be maintained in the star file.

I hope these two tests help you get to the bottom of the glitch in
this exercise.
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK

On Tue, Aug 16, 2022 at 8:40 AM Anna Beck <annamaria.beck0143@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> Dear all,
>
> I have been trying to follow the team-tomo tutorial on HIV, and encountered a problem at the step of conversion from Dynamo to Warp/Relion.
> I have already got some feedback from the Dynamo google group, and my conclusion at this point is that it's Warp that is likely doing something strange at the level of Subtomogram Reconstruction.
>
> Are there any known problems that occur at this step?
>
> Cause all steps before look fine (tomograms reconstructed in Warp are fine, Dynamo averages are fine). But when I convert my dynamo table to .star, reconstruct subtomos in Warp and do the first averaging step in relion - it looks horrible.
>
> Any input would be highly appreciated! I am out of ideas! What else can I check?
>
> Anna
>
>
>
> --
